void.$description: Void — Page canvas, dominant card surface — the near-black that absorbs the eye and lets project imagery carry all visual weight

white.$description: White — Hairline borders, text, link borders, and the 124 link borders form the white wireframe structure of every tile and section

midnight-navy.$description: Midnight Navy — Accent card surface and decorative panels — the only chromatic hue in the system, used sparingly as a deep tonal interruption against the void

cement.$description: Cement — Alternate card surface — a warm desaturated gray that breaks the black/navy rhythm when a tile needs to feel physical or grounded

suisse-intl.$description: Exclusive display and body font — the studio's signature is deploying a weight 600 grotesque at a whisper-small 12–14px for absolutely everything, from navigation to project labels to body copy. This uniform micro-size is anti-convention; most agencies use a dramatic type scale, but HEED treats 12–14px weight 600 as a single visual register, like a single monospace that carries every function.

void-canvas.$description: Surface level 0: Full-bleed page background — the dark room that holds the exhibition

midnight-panel.$description: Surface level 1: Accent project tiles and decorative breaks

cement-tile.$description: Surface level 2: Warm alternate surface for project cards that need physical presence

white-inset.$description: Surface level 3: Inverted project tiles (white photography backgrounds) and image surfaces
